title: Wholesome Saudi Rice Flour Stew
description: A comforting citrusy stew featuring pillowy rice flour pancakes, slow-cooked with fresh vegetables and fragrant herbs for a nostalgic Saudi-American flavor.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk rice flour with water until smooth and set aside.
Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat; add chopped onion and minced garlic, sauté until translucent.
Add sliced carrot and zucchini to the pot and stir for 3 minutes.
Pour in vegetable stock and add the cinnamon stick, bringing the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Stir in the rice flour mixture in one steady pour, whisking continuously to prevent lumps.
Allow the stew to simmer for another 15 minutes, until the mixture thickens into a doughy consistency.
Remove the cinnamon stick and season with salt, pepper, lemon zest, and lemon juice.
Gently fold in chopped fresh coriander and adjust seasoning if needed.
Ladle the stew into individual bowls, drizzle a small amount of olive oil on top, and serve hot.
